unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Change `contentLoader` return type to `Promise<{ sections: DocumentSection[]; hasMore: boolean }>` or make `totalSections` required. Add explicit documentation that `endReached` must be gated on `hasMore === true` or `loadedCount < totalSections`. Add an `isComplete` derived state to the viewer that disables further fetches. This is a correctness issue — without it, the viewer will hammer the API with empty requests after document end.

unresolved_objections.description: Framer Motion 11 is listed at 'shared libs ≤ 50 KB gzip' alongside TanStack Query, Zustand, and Radix. Framer Motion alone is ~32-44KB gzip (even with tree-shaking, `AnimatePresence` + `motion` + `layout` pulls in most of the bundle). TanStack Query v5 is ~12KB, Zustand ~1.5KB, Radix primitives vary but Dialog+Toast+Popover easily 8-15KB. That's already 53-72KB before any other Radix usage. The 50KB budget is busted on day one. This makes the size-limit CI gate a false sense of security — it will either fail immediately or the team will bump the budget to pass, defeating the purpose.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Split the budget into: (a) Framer Motion: ≤ 35KB gzip (isolate in its own chunk, lazy-loaded with route transitions only), (b) TanStack Query + Zustand: ≤ 15KB gzip, (c) Radix primitives: ≤ 15KB gzip. Total shared ≤ 65KB but each has its own gate. Alternatively, evaluate replacing Framer Motion with CSS View Transitions API (supported in Chromium, progressive enhancement for others) for page transitions, and use CSS @keyframes for the remaining layout animations. This would eliminate the single largest discretionary dependency.

unresolved_objections.description: The comment says `snapPoints` can be `number[]` representing 'porcentagens 0-1 ou pixels', but vaul v0.9.x actually expects string snap points like `['148px', '355px']` or fraction strings. The helper `parseSnapPoints(['25vh', '50vh', '92dvh'])` is mentioned but not typed, and the interface says `number[]`. This mismatch between the contract and the actual vaul API will cause runtime errors when developers pass `[0.25, 0.5, 0.92]` and vaul interprets them as pixel values.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Correct the type to `snapPoints?: (string | number)[]` matching vaul's actual API, or create a strongly-typed wrapper: `type SnapPoint = `${number}px` | `${number}vh` | `${number}dvh` | number`. Define and type the `parseSnapPoints` utility explicitly in the contract. Pin vaul version range in the ADR to avoid silent API breaks.

unresolved_objections.description: The contract says 'SearchSurface gerencia AbortController internamente' but `onSearch` receives an `AbortSignal`. This means the parent provides the callback but the child controls the signal lifecycle — the parent has no way to cancel from the outside (e.g., user navigates away, component unmounts). Also, the 300ms debounce value is hardcoded in a comment with no prop to override. For a professional tool, power users may want shorter debounce; for slow connections, longer. The fixed value is a UX assumption baked into the contract.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Add `debounceMs?: number` prop with default 300. Add `abortControllerRef?: MutableRefObject<AbortController | null>` so the parent can imperatively cancel (or at minimum, document that unmount cleanup aborts in-flight requests via useEffect cleanup). Alternatively, move AbortController management to the parent via a hook `useSearchAbort()` and pass the signal down.

unresolved_objections.description: Single-character shortcuts (`j`, `k`, `/`, `t`, `f`) will fire inside contenteditable elements, custom inputs, or any future rich-text area. The contract mentions checking `event.target` for input/textarea but doesn't mention `contenteditable`, `role='textbox'`, or elements with `isContentEditable`. This is a known footgun — Gmail-style shortcuts require a more robust guard. Also, `j`/`k` conflict with common browser extensions (Vimium, etc.) and there's no mechanism to disable or rebind shortcuts.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: The `useKeyboardShortcuts` hook must check: `event.target.tagName` not in `['INPUT', 'TEXTAREA', 'SELECT']` AND `!event.target.isContentEditable` AND `!event.target.closest('[role="textbox"]')`. Add a `shortcuts: Partial<Record<ShortcutAction, string>>` to user preferences for rebinding. Add an escape hatch: if any modal/sheet is open (z-index layer active), suppress document-level shortcuts.

unresolved_objections.description: Four font families (IBM Plex Sans 400+600, Literata 400+400i, JetBrains Mono) means at minimum 5 font files on critical path (even with subset). Each preloaded font adds a connection and blocks rendering. On 3G, preloading 4 fonts will blow the 2.5s LCP target. The `size-adjust: 103%` for Literata→Georgia is stated but not verified — size-adjust values depend on the exact metrics of both fonts and 103% is likely wrong (Literata has significantly different ascender/descender metrics than Georgia).
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: 1) Use `fontaine` or `@capsizecss/metrics` to compute exact size-adjust, ascent-override, descent-override, and line-gap-override for the Literata→Georgia fallback. Don't guess 103%. 2) Limit critical preload to IBM Plex Sans 400 and 600 only (used for UI chrome). Literata should use `font-display: swap` without preload — the serif fallback (Georgia) is close enough that CLS will be minimal with proper overrides, and the document body is below-fold on most pages. 3) JetBrains Mono should be `font-display: optional` and loaded only when DocumentViewer mounts (not on initial page load).

unresolved_objections.description: The z-index scale jumps by 100 (0, 100, 200, 300, 400, 500). This leaves room for intermediate values, which is good, but the `isolation: isolate` directive per component will create new stacking contexts that make these values meaningless across boundaries. If AppShell sidebar has `isolation: isolate` and a dropdown inside it uses `var(--z-dropdown)`, the dropdown won't escape the sidebar's stacking context. The architecture needs to specify WHICH components create stacking contexts and which are portalized to escape them.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Document explicitly: (a) Components rendered via React Portal (sheet, modal, toast, dropdown): these escape stacking contexts and use z-index tokens. (b) Components with `isolation: isolate`: AppShell regions only (sidebar, main), for internal layering. (c) Add to the z-index rule: 'Portalized components MUST use React.createPortal to document.body to ensure z-index tokens are effective.' Add this to the component contracts as a rendering requirement.

unresolved_objections.description: API ambígua: `onSearch` retorna `Promise<void>` mas não define como os resultados chegam à UI. Se é via TanStack Query, o callback não deveria retornar Promise<void>. Se é via props de resultados, falta definir `results: SearchResult[]`. O padrão atual quebra o fluxo de dados.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Separar responsabilidades: SearchSurface recebe `results` e `status` como props, gerencia debounce interno, e chama `onSearch(query, filters)` (sem AbortSignal, sem Promise — TanStack Query gerencia async/cancel). OU: SearchSurface é um controlled component que recebe tudo via props, incluindo `onSearchChange: (query: string) => void`.

unresolved_objections.description: Três problemas na interface: (1) `scrollToMatch` como prop sugere callback, mas descrição diz 'dispara scrollToIndex' — isso é imperativeHandle, não prop. (2) `offset/limit` para streaming pode cortar no meio de uma seção lógica (ex: artigo cortado no meio de seus incisos). (3) `matchIndex` em `searchMatches` é ambíguo — índice da seção ou índice do match dentro do documento?
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: (1) Expor via useImperativeHandle: `ref.current.scrollToMatch(matchIndex: number)`. (2) Usar `sectionId` como cursor em vez de offset, ou garantir que contentLoader retorne seções completas. (3) Renomear para `occurrenceIndex` ou `documentMatchIndex` com comentário explicativo.

unresolved_objections.description: Budget de ≤50KB gzip para shared libs é irrealista. Framer Motion 11 (~25-30KB gzip) + TanStack Query v5 (~13KB) + Radix primitives (Dialog ~8KB, Select ~6KB, etc) + Zustand (~3KB) = ~55-65KB mínimo antes de qualquer outra dependência. O budget vai falhar no primeiro dia.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Ajustar para ≤80KB gzip para shared libs, OU remover Framer Motion (usar CSS transitions + vaul para tudo) e manter 50KB. Documentar trade-off explícito: animações ricas vs bundle size.

unresolved_objections.description: Light theme não define variantes completas (`-hover`, `-active`, `-muted`) para `--color-secondary` e cores semânticas (`--color-danger`, `--color-success`, `--color-info`), enquanto dark theme define parcialmente. Inconsistência gera bugs quando componentes usam essas variantes.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Completar todas as variantes de cores no light theme, ou documentar explicitamente que componentes só usam variantes no dark theme e devem usar cor base em light theme (mas isso quebra consistência de código).

unresolved_objections.description: 10 dias para: Vite+React+TS strict, 3 temas completos, preload de fontes + CLS mitigation, ESLint/Prettier/Stylelint com regras custom, AppShell, Zustand+IndexedDB, TanStack Query, ErrorBoundary, Vitest, Ladle, Sentry é irrealista. Qualquer desenvolvedor real vai estourar esse prazo em 2-3x.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Quebrar em 3 semanas realistas: Semana 1 (foundation: Vite, TS, tokens dark-only, linters), Semana 2 (state management, ErrorBoundary, AppShell), Semana 3 (temas light/high-contrast, testes, Sentry). Ou reduzir escopo do MVP.

unresolved_objections.description: Blueprint menciona utilitário `parseSnapPoints(['25vh', '50vh', '92dvh'])` mas não o define em lugar nenhum. É código que não existe, criando dívida técnica imediata.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Adicionar implementação do utilitário em 'Seção 4.5' ou em nova seção 'Utilities': `function parseSnapPoints(points: string[]): number[] { return points.map(p => { const match = p.match(/(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)(vh|dvh|px)/); if (!match) throw new Error('Invalid snap point'); const value = parseFloat(match[1]); const unit = match[2]; return unit === 'px' ? value : value * window.innerHeight / 100; }); }`

unresolved_objections.description: Atalhos `j`/`k` para navegação de seção podem conflitar com navegação por letra em leitores de tela (JAWS/NVDA usam letras para pular para elementos). Pode quebrar a11y em vez de melhorar.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Documentar que atalhos só são ativados quando foco está no DocumentViewer (não global), ou usar modificadores: `Alt+j`/`Alt+k`. Adicionar teste manual com NVDA no roadmap da Fase 1.
